response: This function uses a dynamic programming tabulation approach to compute the length of the longest common subsequence (LCS) between two input strings: it constructs a 2D DP table where each entry `dp[i][j]` stores the LCS length of the first `i` characters of string `a` and first `j` characters of string `b`, fills the table by using either the diagonal DP value plus one for matching characters or the maximum of adjacent top/left DP values for non-matching characters, then returns the final full LCS length at the bottom-right corner of the table.
